findings.title: Warm-409 union-merge resurrects a media item the user just deleted (photo / document / checklist to-do / saved place)
findings.rootCause: `_mergeMediaField(serverItems, pendingItems)` (media.ts:62) builds `out = [...serverItems]` then appends only local items whose key is NOT already on the server. On a warm write, `persistTripMedia` → `_postTripMedia` sends the FULL post-delete snapshot; the server returns 409 with `current` = live media that STILL CONTAINS the deleted item (because the peer's write didn't delete it). The merge keys by stable `id`, sees the deleted item's id present in `cur`, keeps the server copy, and the local snapshot (which OMITS it = the delete) loses. The merged result is reflected into STATE (media.ts:149-158) and retried, persisting the resurrected item. The code comment at media.ts:42-46 asserts union-by-key 'can't resurrect a peer device's delete' and is only sound for the cold-window ADD-only case — but `_postTripMedia`'s 409 loop runs for EVERY warm write, including deletes, where the assumption is false.

findings.title: Warm-409 union-merge silently discards a concurrent EDIT to an existing media item (checklist toggle, doc rename, photo day-reassign, place forManual/forAI toggle)
findings.rootCause: Same `_mergeMediaField` (media.ts:62): for an item key present on BOTH sides (the edited item retains its `id`), the server's copy is taken (`out = [...serverItems]`) and the local edited copy is dropped because its key is already `seen`. So 'server wins ties' silently overwrites the local field-level edit. Edits route through `upsertTrip` → `persistTripMedia` → `_postTripMedia`, hitting the same 409 merge. Reorder (tripMediaModals.ts:658-661) is also lost — server item order comes back first, the local reordered array is discarded.

findings.title: HEIC→JPEG re-encode exception fallback writes raw HEIC bytes into a .jpg file → broken image served as image/jpeg
findings.rootCause: For `.heic`/`.heif`, `filename`/`out_path` are rewritten to `.jpg` at media.py:232-233 BEFORE the encode. The encode `img.save(out_path, format='JPEG', exif=b'')` (media.py:295) can raise — most realistically `OSError: cannot write mode RGBA/P/LA as JPEG` because there is no `img.convert('RGB')` before the JPEG save — and the broad `except Exception:` at media.py:313-315 then does `file.save(out_path)`, persisting the ORIGINAL HEIC bytes under the `.jpg` name. The happy-path fix (rewriting the extension) made the fallback worse, because the fallback no longer matches the file's true format.

findings.title: Per-item uploaded files are orphaned on disk on photo/doc removal, cover swap, and document 'Replace'
findings.rootCause: Disk cleanup (`collect_trip_upload_paths` + `delete_upload_files`) is wired ONLY into whole-trip and whole-day delete. `update_trip_media` (trips.py:698) just overwrites the photos_json/documents_json column and never diffs the old array to rm the now-unreferenced file; the cover-swap path (trip.ts:463) overwrites `trip.coverUrl` with no cleanup of the prior cover. The 'Replace' document flow (tripMediaModals.ts:856) uploads a new file and overwrites the url, orphaning the old one.

findings.title: Media POST does not validate/sanitize item URLs — arbitrary src/url (incl. javascript:) persisted into trip media
findings.rootCause: `update_trip_media` (trips.py:719-732) validates only that each field is a list and within the 512KB cap — it never validates the items' URL scheme or ownership, unlike the expense path which runs `validate_upload_url` on `receipt_url`. The client `addTripPhoto`/`addTripDocument` (tripMedia.ts:113, :95) store the raw user-typed string with no scheme allowlist. Cross-user /static/uploads exfiltration is separately blocked by serve_upload's membership gate, but javascript:/data: schemes and external-link abuse are not filtered anywhere.
